# BRC-20 | Bitcoin Universe Protocol Reference

> Human docs: index.html
> Field reference: reference.html
> Network: Bitcoin
> Classification: Ordinals fungible token experiment

## Summary

A compact JSON inscription convention for deploying, minting, and transferring fungible token balances through Ordinals-aware infrastructure.

## State model

Indexer-derived balances from JSON text inscriptions and their Ordinal transfers.

## Lifecycle

1. Deploy ticker
2. Mint inscription
3. Create transferable
4. Send Ordinal

## Operations

- Deploy: Registers the ticker, maximum supply, optional mint limit, and optional precision.
- Mint: Credits the first owner of a valid mint inscription, subject to remaining supply and limit.
- Transfer: Reserves an amount in a transferable inscription. Sending that inscription settles the transfer.

## Safety

1. Treat the original BRC-20 specification as an experiment. Indexer interpretation and wallet support determine practical interoperability.
2. Transfers are two-step. Do not send a transferable inscription until the expected amount is shown as transferable.
3. Use an Ordinals-aware receiving and sending flow. Accidental UTXO selection can strand or mis-handle inscriptions.

## Sources

- BRC-20 experiment: https://docs.bitcoints.org/brc-20
- Universe Inscribe: https://inscribe.bitcoinuniverse.io
